title: Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary
tagline: Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Todgarh Raoli Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Todgarh Raoli Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Rajasthan, India. Spread over the Ajmer, Pali and Rajsamand districts of the state, it was established in 1983. It occupies about 495 km2 of tropical deciduous forests and grassland. Major wildlife includes leopard, wild boar, chinkara, common langur, sloth bears and Indian wolf.

topPlaces.name: Siriyari
topPlaces.description: Siriyari is a small town situated in State of Rajasthan, District Pali. It is situated on State Highway 62 connecting Sojat, Sojat Road, Siriyari, Phulad, Jojawar.

items.note: Siriyari is a small town situated in State of Rajasthan, District Pali. It is situated on State Highway 62 connecting Sojat, Sojat Road, Siriyari, Phulad, Jojawar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jodhpur Airport (106 km) and the nearest railway station is Jodhpur Junction (111 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Todgarh-Raoli Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
